message: Local hygiene gate selects files from the git index (git ls-files) but scans worktree content via os.ReadFile and skips worktree-deleted files, so staged content that diverges from the worktree is not what is verified. A staged secret whose worktree copy was edited or deleted afterward passes `make heurema-hygiene` locally and gets committed; CI only catches it after the secret has been pushed to the remote. Scanning staged blob content (e.g. git show :<path> or git grep --cached) would close the gap.
evidence: heuremaFiles (main.go:102) lists index entries; scan (main.go:121-124) reads filepath.Join(root, f) from the worktree and continues on fs.ErrNotExist. Acceptance criteria as worded (consider git ls-files plus staged-added files) are met, so advisory.

message: Slack detector regexp xox[abprs]- omits the real Slack token prefixes xoxc- (client/browser), xoxd- (browser cookie), and xoxe- (rotated tokens), so such tokens in a committed run record pass the gate despite the family-level acceptance criterion for Slack xox tokens. Widening the class (e.g. xox[a-z]-) preserves the 10+-char plausibility requirement and the bare-prefix doc-example behavior.
evidence: main.go:55 pattern \bxox[abprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{10,}; xoxc/xoxd/xoxe are documented Slack credential prefixes not in the class. No instance in the tracked .heurema tree today (gate clean over 1685 files).

message: No automated test exercises the command-level nonzero exit path. Acceptance criteria 5-6 say 'make heurema-hygiene exits nonzero when...', but tests only assert scan()'s finding counts; the findings>0 -> os.Exit(1) mapping and the exit-2 error branches (repoRoot failure, heuremaFiles git failure, non-ENOENT read error) are uncovered (package coverage 54.2%, all uncovered statements are in main/repoRoot/error branches). A regression dropping the os.Exit(1) would pass make check and the gate's clean-tree validation, silently disarming the leak gate.
evidence: main.go:84-88 maps findings to exit codes; main_test.go tests scan()/heuremaFiles() only, never runs the binary or main(); gate validation command_002 ran make heurema-hygiene on a clean tree (exit 0), so the dirty/nonzero path is exercised nowhere.

message: This change implements two 'Hardening / cleanup' backlog items — 'govulncheck in CI' and 'Committed run-record hygiene gate' — but docs/backlog.md is not updated, so both still read as open to-do items. Repo convention marks entries shipped in the implementing PR (M24.1's PR rewrote its own Slice 4 entry to '(M24.1, shipped)').
evidence: docs/backlog.md:361-367 still lists both items under 'Hardening / cleanup'; gate change summary shows docs/backlog.md untouched; git show 7da01d5 demonstrates the mark-shipped-in-PR convention.

message: Pre-existing: docs/install.md:53, README.md:224, and AGENTS.md:27 describe `make check` as test + vet + git diff --check, omitting the deadcode gate that `check: test vet deadcode` runs. The install.md line was re-aligned by this change but the stale description was carried over.
evidence: Makefile target is `check: test vet deadcode` + `git diff --check` (unchanged by this run); deadcode was added in M11.3 per docs/backlog.md:581. Pre-existing, advisory.

message: `heurema-hygiene` scans the worktree copy of index-selected files, so it can miss secrets that are staged for commit.
evidence: `heuremaFiles` selects files from `git ls-files`, but `scan` uses `os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(root, f))` and skips missing worktree files. A staged `.heurema` file containing a leak can be edited clean or deleted in the worktree before `make heurema-hygiene`, causing the gate to pass while the staged commit still contains the leak.

message: The hygiene scanner selects staged .heurema paths from the Git index but scans worktree bytes, so staged secret content can be missed when the worktree differs from the index.
evidence: heuremaFiles uses `git ls-files` to include staged-added paths, but scan reads `os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(root, f))`; the committed/staged content is not read via the index.

message: The hygiene scanner silently skips git-index .heurema files that are missing from the worktree, so a staged-added file can still be committed while the gate reports clean if the worktree copy was deleted before running the target.
evidence: heuremaFiles builds the scan set with git ls-files, but scan reads filepath.Join(root, f) from the worktree and continues on fs.ErrNotExist at lines 121-123. A staged-added .heurema file remains in the index and commit contents even when the worktree path is absent.

message: docs/backlog.md still lists the newly implemented govulncheck CI job and committed run-record hygiene gate as open backlog work.
evidence: The backlog lines 361-367 ask to add govulncheck in CI and a run-record hygiene gate, while this run added make vuln, make heurema-hygiene, and CI steps for both.
